pattern: Only two families of arrangement are ever reachable this way — cyclic rotations of the sorted order, and cyclic rotations of the fully reversed order — so build a tiny graph over just those arrangements (linked by 'rotate' and 'reverse' moves) and read the shortest distance to sorted straight off it.
hints: Try the two allowed moves starting from the sorted array itself and see which arrangements come back as reachable — only cyclic rotations of the ascending order and cyclic rotations of the fully descending order ever show up, nothing else.

Because every reachable arrangement belongs to one of those two small families, you can classify any input by which family it matches and which rotation offset it sits at, and immediately say -1 if it matches neither.

Build a small graph with one node per rotation offset in each of the two families (rotating moves you to the neighboring offset in the same family, reversing moves you to a specific offset in the other family), and run a shortest-path search on that tiny graph once to answer every offset at once.

edge_inputs.description: the smallest non-trivial permutation with two elements swapped
the fully reversed permutation, solvable by a single reverse
a rotation of the sorted order by a single step
a permutation that is neither a rotation of ascending nor of descending order

stress.description: rotations of the ascending order across a range of shift amounts and lengths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    shift = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    nums = [(i + shift) % n for i in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: rotations of the descending order across a range of shift amounts and lengths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    base = list(range(n - 1, -1, -1))
    shift = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    nums = base[shift:] + base[:shift]
    return [nums]

stress.description: random shuffles, almost always unreachable and expected to answer -1
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
